subject: attach functions
old: attach functions work with any struct xdp_program object
new: attach functions only work with struct xdp_program objects that have not yet been loaded into the kernel
truth: Stop passing already-kernel-loaded xdp_program objects to xdp_program__attach() or xdp_program__attach_multi(); they will fail.
quote: the attach functions only work with struct xdp_program objects that have not yet been loaded into the kernel

from_fact: attach functions work with any struct xdp_program object
attach functions only work with struct xdp_program objects that have not yet been loaded into the kernel

subject: xdp_program__attach_multi()
old: can attach multiple programs incrementally to an interface that already has an XDP program
new: if kernel lacks incremental attach support (pre-5.10), the only way to run multiple programs is to attach them all at once with xdp_program__attach_multi()
truth: On kernels older than 5.10, you cannot add a program to an interface that already has an XDP program; you must detach the existing program first or use xdp_program__attach_multi() to attach all programs simultaneously.
quote: If the kernel support for incremental attach (merged in kernel 5.10) is missing, the only way to actually run multiple programs on a single interface is to attach them all at the same time with xdp_program__attach_multi()

from_fact: can attach multiple programs incrementally to an interface that already has an xdp program
if kernel lacks incremental attach support (pre-5.10), the only way to run multiple programs is to attach them all at once with xdp_program__attach_multi()

subject: AF_XDP socket helper functions
truth: Stop using AF_XDP socket helper functions from libbpf; use libxdp instead as they have been moved there and will be removed from libbpf in the 1.0 release.
quote: this functionality used to reside in libbpf, but has now been moved over to libxdp as it is a better fit for this library. As of the 1.0 release of libbpf, the AF_XDP socket support will be removed and all future development will be performed in libxdp instead.

subject: xsk_socket__create_shared()
old: could be used without privileges if an AF_XDP socket was created beforehand and passed to a non-privileged process
new: still requires privileges for AF_XDP socket creation
truth: xsk_socket__create_shared() still requires privileges for AF_XDP socket creation, unlike xsk_socket__create() which can use a pre-created socket passed from a privileged process.
quote: xsk_socket__create_shared() would still require privileges for AF_XDP socket creation.

from_fact: could be used without privileges if an af_xdp socket was created beforehand and passed to a non-privileged process
still requires privileges for af_xdp socket creation

subject: xsk_umem__get_data() in unaligned mode
old: could use addr straight from the Rx descriptor to get packet data
new: must use xsk_umem__extract_addr(), xsk_umem__extract_offset(), and xsk_umem__add_offset_to_addr() because the offset is carried in the upper 16 bits of addr
truth: In unaligned mode, you cannot use the addr straight from the Rx descriptor; you must use xsk_umem__extract_addr(), xsk_umem__extract_offset(), and xsk_umem__add_offset_to_addr() to get the packet data pointer.
quote: In the default aligned mode, you can get the addr variable straight from the Rx descriptor. But in unaligned mode, you need to use the three last function below as the offset used is carried in the upper 16 bits of the addr. Therefore, you cannot use the addr straight from the descriptor in the unaligned case.

from_fact: could use addr straight from the rx descriptor to get packet data
must use xsk_umem__extract_addr(), xsk_umem__extract_offset(), and xsk_umem__add_offset_to_addr() because the offset is carried in the upper 16 bits of addr

subject: xsk_ring_prod__needs_wakeup()
old: poll() was the only way to check if wakeup is needed
new: xsk_ring_prod__needs_wakeup() returns true, you must call recvmsg(), sendto(), or poll() depending on direction
truth: When xsk_ring_prod__needs_wakeup() returns true, you must call recvmsg() for receiving or sendto() for sending instead of always using poll(), because poll() is slower.
quote: If this function returns true, then you need to call recvmsg(), sendto(), or poll() depending on the situation. recvmsg() if you are *receiving*, or sendto() if you are *sending*. poll() can be used for both cases and provide the ability to sleep too, as with any other socket. But note that poll is a slower operation than the other two.

from_fact: poll() was the only way to check if wakeup is needed
xsk_ring_prod__needs_wakeup() returns true, you must call recvmsg(), sendto(), or poll() depending on direction

subject: bpf_set_link_xdp_fd()
truth: Stop using bpf_set_link_xdp_fd(); use bpf_xdp_attach() instead.

subject: bpf_get_link_xdp_id()
truth: Stop using bpf_get_link_xdp_id(); use bpf_xdp_query_id() instead.

subject: AF_XDP APIs from xsk.h in libbpf
old: AF_XDP APIs from xsk.h in libbpf
truth: Stop using AF_XDP-related APIs from libbpf's xsk.h; they have been consolidated into libxdp
quote: AF_XDP-related parts of libbpf (APIs from xsk.h) were consolidated into libxdp.

from_fact: af_xdp apis from xsk.h in libbpf

subject: bpf_set_link_xdp_fd_opts()
truth: Stop using bpf_set_link_xdp_fd_opts(); use bpf_xdp_attach() instead.

subject: bpf_get_link_xdp_info()
truth: Stop using bpf_get_link_xdp_info(); use bpf_xdp_query() instead.

subject: AF_XDP (xsk.h) API
old: xsk.h AF_XDP functions (xsk_umem__fd, xsk_socket__fd, xsk_setup_xdp_prog, xsk_socket__update_xskmap)
truth: Stop using the xsk.h AF_XDP API in libbpf; it has been removed and moved to libxdp (link with libxdp instead).
quote: This whole API has been deprecated and moved to libxdp that can be found at https://github.com/xdp-project/xdp-tools. The APIs are exactly the same so it should just be linking with libxdp instead of libbpf for this set of functionality.

from_fact: xsk.h af_xdp functions (xsk_umem__fd, xsk_socket__fd, xsk_setup_xdp_prog, xsk_socket__update_xskmap)
